ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03311069 covers uttapam as a class; onion-topped variant is not separately coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers fermented cereal pancakes broadly; no specific uttapam code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for uttapam or South Indian fermented pancakes. None assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium onion uttapam piece, approximately 120 g, as typically served at a South Indian restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Uttapam (Uthappam) — Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, ICMR-NIN
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods reports plain uttapam (uthappam) per 100 g. Values adapted for onion-topped variant: onion topping adds minimal calories (~5 kcal/100 g adjustment) and negligible macro change; core nutrient profile treated as equivalent to plain uttapam from ICMR-NIN. Sodium adjusted upward slightly to reflect typical restaurant preparation with added salt.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of South Indian fermented foods: idli and dosa — Wolever et al. and University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for fermented rice-lentil preparations
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for onion uttapam exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 51 is estimated from the closest analogous fermented rice-lentil preparations (idli GI ~46–54, dosa GI ~50–55) listed in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Wolever TMS et al., Am J Clin Nutr). Uttapam shares the same fermented rice-urad dal batter base as idli and dosa; the onion topping does not materially alter glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (51 × 36.6) / 100 ≈ 18.7, using 120 g serving with 30.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from added salt in restaurant preparation
hypertension.guidance: Onion uttapam contains approximately 310 mg sodium per 100 g; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one piece and avoid additional high-sodium accompaniments such as sambar or pickle to keep total meal sodium within recommended limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index and significant carbohydrate load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 51 (low-moderate) and a glycemic load of ~18.7 per standard serving, onion uttapam causes a moderate postprandial glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume one piece with a protein-rich accompaniment such as sambar or dal to blunt the glycemic response and monitor portion size carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, cholesterol-free, and plant-based composition
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Onion uttapam is low in saturated fat (~0.6 g/100 g), contains no cholesterol, and is made from plant-based ingredients; it is a heart-friendly staple when prepared with minimal oil and served without high-fat coconut chutney in large quantities.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Onion uttapam (also spelled uthappam or oothappam) is a thick, soft South Indian pancake made from a fermented batter of rice and urad dal (black gram lentil), topped with finely chopped raw onions before cooking on a hot griddle with a small amount of oil. Unlike the thinner dosa, uttapam is cooked slowly on both sides, resulting in a spongy, slightly tangy interior with a lightly crisp exterior and a mild sweetness from the caramelised onion topping. It is a staple breakfast and tiffin item across Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, and Kerala, and is widely served in South Indian restaurants throughout Singapore, where it is popular among the Indian diaspora community. Nutritionally, onion uttapam provides moderate energy (~175 kcal/100 g), a useful combination of rice-derived carbohydrates and lentil-derived protein (~5.2 g/100 g), and is naturally gluten-free, vegan, and low in saturated fat. The fermentation process improves digestibility and bioavailability of B vitamins. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 51 (low-moderate), making it a more glycemically favourable grain-based option compared to plain white rice. Clinically, the moderate sodium content (~310 mg/100 g) from restaurant preparation warrants attention for hypertensive individuals, while the moderate glycemic load (~18.7 per serving) requires portion awareness for those managing Type 2 Diabetes. It is typically served with sambar (a lentil-vegetable soup) and coconut chutney.
common_user_queries: How many calories in onion uttapam?
What is the difference between dosa and uttapam?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN plain uttapam data, which is the highest-priority approved source for Indian dishes. The onion-topped variant is not separately listed; adaptation is minor and well-justified. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using idli/dosa as the closest fermented rice-lentil analogues. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact match for onion uttapam exists in any approved primary source.
notes: 1. Cuisine corrected from 'Malaysian' (input) to 'South Indian': uttapam is a South Indian dish of Tamil origin, served at the listed restaurant 'Singapore Banana Leaf Restaurant' which is a South Indian establishment. The cuisine_tags in the input ('Malaysian', 'Noodle', 'Fried Rice', 'Roti Prata') appear to be platform-level category tags for the restaurant rather than dish-specific tags and have not been carried forward. 2. Nutrient values sourced from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (plain uttapam/uthappam); minor adaptation for onion topping. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not separately reported for uttapam in ICMR-NIN. 4. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for this dish. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for this dish. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for this dish. 7. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for this dish. 8. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as uttapam is a plant-based, minimally processed food with no industrial trans fats; confirmed negligible per ICMR-NIN. 9. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as uttapam contains no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN. 10. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as uttapam is a vegan plant-based food containing no animal products; naturally zero. 11. GI estimated at 51 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous fermented rice-lentil preparations (idli, dosa); no direct GI measurement for uttapam found. 12. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean dish context (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the dish is served in Singapore. 13. FoodOn FOODON_03311069 and FoodEx2 A0CKP used as closest parent codes; no exact ontology codes for onion uttapam exist.
